business.headline: Reliance buys 5 million barrels of Iranian oil after US waiver, sources sayThe development comes after the US administration announced a 30-day waiver on certain sanctions related to Iranian oil.

India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand: RTIAn RTI reply has revealed that India's oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand, even as global supply risks rise amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

Only 40% women invest beyond FDs and gold: Why the gap still exists?Indian women are known for their strong saving habits, but when it comes to investing beyond fixed deposits and gold, many still hold back. What is stopping them from taking that next step?
Markets bounce back, Sensex up 1,300 points, banks leadThe Sensex surged 1,372 points to close at 74,068.45, while the Nifty 50 rose 399.75 points to settle at 22,912.40, reclaiming the 22,900 mark.

Markets in panic mode, gold and silver also hit: What's left for investors?Since the Iran war began, the Sensex has fallen about 10.6%, while the Nifty has dropped nearly 11%. At the same time, gold and silver have also declined, leaving investors with limited places to hide.

Form 16 gone from April 2026: What will your employer give you instead?A key document for salaried taxpayers is set to change. Starting April 2026, Form 16 may no longer be issued, with a new format stepping in to reshape how your income details are reported.

Goldman Sachs cuts India growth forecast to 5.9%, flags rate hike risksThe revision follows changes in Goldman's assumptions on oil prices and supply disruptions. Higher crude prices remain a major risk for India, which relies heavily on imports.

8th Pay Commission: When will your salary rise and how much can you getCentral government employees are eagerly waiting for the 8th Pay Commission update. While a decent salary hike is expected, the bigger question remains, i.e., when will the revised pay actually start coming in?

Swiggy hikes platform fee to Rs 17.58 after Zomato, food orders get costlierFood delivery bills are inching up again. Swiggy has increased its platform fee soon after Zomato, meaning customers will now pay more each time they order online.

Bought gold during Iran war? Here's why it's not giving you returnsGold and silver prices have fallen despite ongoing war tensions, which is unusual as precious metals are typically seen as a safe investment during crises like war.

Kotak Mahindra Bank shares rise: Why is the stock gaining today?The sharp rise reflects a recovery after recent weakness, but investors should watch for consistency in momentum as broader market trends and foreign investor flows continue to influence banking stocks.

Gold falls Rs 2,500, silver down Rs 8,000: Why are safe-haven assets tumbling?Gold and silver prices are falling even as the West Asia conflict continues. Here's why safe-haven assets are losing appeal and what it means for investors.

Sensex rises 1,700 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Sensex and Nifty rebounded sharply on Tuesday as tensions eased, oil prices fell and investors returned to banking stocks after Monday's sharp fall.
Sensex, Nifty jump as US delays Iran grid strike; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 993.75 points to 73,690.14, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 319.20 points to 22,831.85 as of 9:29 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening today: Will stock markets crash again amid Iran war?Early signals show a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,878 at around 8:40 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open about 1.6% higher compared to Monday's close of 22,512.65.

$580 million bets before Trump's Iran remark. Coincidence or insider trading?Perfectly timed oil and equity trades worth $580 million before US President Donald Trump claimed "productive talks" with Iran and paused strikes, later denied by Tehran, are raising insider trading concerns.

IndiGo appoints former Air India Express CEO Aloke Singh as Strategy ChiefBetween December 3 and 5, 2025, IndiGo cancelled 2,507 flights and delayed 1,852 others, affecting over three lakh passengers nationwide. The disruptions were linked to challenges in implementing revised Flight Duty Time Limitation (FDTL) norms for pilots.

Wall Street rallies as Trump delays Iran strikesThe positive sentiment followed Trump's statement that the US would postpone planned strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for five days.

Sebi eases trade rules for foreign investors amid rising market outflowsUntil now, investors had to settle each buy and sell trade separately. This meant higher funding needs and more complex transactions. With net settlement, they can offset buy and sell positions, reducing the amount of money required for each cycle.
Oil prices fall over 13% as Trump pauses Iran energy strikesOil prices jumped in the morning with Brent nearing $113, but later fell to around $100.34, down 6.2%, later in the day.

entertainment.headline: Kantara row: Ranveer Singh to apologise in court, visit Chamundi temple Bollywood star Ranveer Singh is set to apologise in court and visit the Chamundi temple following allegations of derogatory remarks in the film Kantara Chapter 1. The Karnataka High Court emphasised the responsibility of public figures to respect religious sentiments.

Is Badshah married? Video of rapper and actor Isha Rikhi goes viral A video and photos showing rapper Badshah and actor Isha Rikhi participating in wedding rituals have sparked widespread speculation about a possible marriage. However, there is no official confirmation from Badshah or his team, leaving the true nature of the event unclear in Indian media circles.

In Arijit Singh's song from Bhooth Bangla, Akshay-Wamiqa's breezy romance. Watch Akshay Kumar and Wamiqa Gabbi's romantic track 'Tu Hi Disda' from Bhooth Bangla offers a soothing, visually rich glimpse into the film.

Dhurandhar 2 actor Manav Gohil says he didn't audition for the film, talks success Manav Gohil reflected on the massive success of Dhurandhar 2, admitting the scale of its box office impact still hasn't sunk in. From a last-minute casting call to sharing screen space with Ranveer Singh and R Madhavan, the actor opened up about his journey.

RGV reacts to Aishwarya Rai's comeback rumours in Sarkar 4, discusses film's scale Ram Gopal Varma teased a bigger cinematic world for his upcoming film Sarkar 4. He also addressed speculations about Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's casting in the film.

Karan Johar hugs Yami Gautam at event, calls her one of his favourite actors At a recent event, Karan Johar and Yami Gautam shared a warm hug. Their camaraderie amid the announcement of Yami's new film Nayyi Navelli have sparked conversations across social media.

India's global pop group era begins: BTS' label HYBE announces nationwide auditions BTS' agency, HYBE, launches India-wide auditions to discover and train the next global pop act, marking a cultural shift in how Indian talent is scouted and developed.

Pics: Priyanka Chopra joins Anne Hathaway, Dua Lipa and Jake Gyllenhaal in Milan Priyanka Chopra attended a high-profile jewellery event in Milan alongside stars like Anne Hathaway and Dua Lipa. She posed for photos and mingled with fellow celebrities at the event.

Understanding Major Iqbal: Why Dhurandhar's chilling villain loses edge by climax Arjun Rampal's Major Iqbal is equal parts menacing and vulnerable. Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge gives him the kind of arc that breaks the usual Bollywood template of a villain, and yet just when it reaches a high, the film does something unthinkable.

Rashmika-Vijay's wedding planner shares note, calls them super creative couple Wedding planner Priya Maganti shared insights into Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's intimate wedding, calling it a deeply personal and emotional experience.

Miley Cyrus sparkles as Hannah Montana at premiere, poses with fiance Maxx Morando Miley Cyrus walked the Hannah Montana anniversary special red carpet with fiance Maxx Morando and family in Hollywood. The special, Hannahversary, is set to premiere online on March 24.

He explained why Ranveer Singh's performance as Hamza feels more human than typical 'superhero' portrayals.

Actor Naveen Kaushik reacts after Dhruv Rathee calls Dhurandhar 2 brain rot Naveen Kaushik responded to Dhruv Rathee's criticism of Dhurandhar 2. He urged audiences not to turn the film into a source of hate despite differing opinions.

Reacher star Alan Ritchson accused of assaulting neighbour over bike noise Actor Alan Ritchson is facing scrutiny after his neighbour alleged a violent altercation over dirt bike noise in Tennessee. His neighbour, Ronnie Taylor, claimed in a recent interview that he was punched by him multiple times.

Dhurandhar 2: Aditya Dhar gave Rakesh Bedi his best comic role ever, with a catch Rakesh Bedi's Jameel Jamali in Dhurandhar: The Revenge steals every frame with comic expressions and quirky dialogues, providing relief amid high-octane drama and gore. The actor's job, though, runs deeper in the script.

The action-thriller, starring Ranveer Singh, Arjun Rampal, R Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sanjay Dutt among others, follows Hamza's mission against terrorism.

The rumours of a third instalment to the hit action thriller grew after a fake end-credit clip and director Aditya Dhar's post on end credits.

Dhurandhar 2: Ranveer Singh's best moments amid gore are ones that let him cry Ranveer Singh delivered a career-defining performance as an action hero in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Four powerful scenes highlighted the versatile actor's unmatched emotional depth.

Valerie Perrine dies at 82, fans remember Superman actor's legacy Veteran actor Valerie Perrine has died at 82 after a long battle with Parkinson's disease. She was known for her roles in Lenny and the Superman films.

Amid this, Pakistan has rushed to mediate between the US and Iran and bring an end to the war. It has positioned itself as the lead mediator. Why does Pakistan want to mediate the US-Iran war so badly?

Live: Iran names new security body chief after Ali Larijani's death in strikesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict is in its fourth week with no let-up, even as tentative signs of de-escalation emerge alongside continued military posturing. Iran launched multiple waves of missiles at Israel on Monday, setting off air raid sirens in Tel Aviv and beyond, even as US President Donald Trump delayed a planned strike on Iranian energy infrastructure citing "productive" talks with Tehran. The attacks, which caused damage to homes in northern Israel but no reported deaths, came amid heightened tensions after Iran moved to effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy route. Meanwhile, at a roundtable in Tennessee, Trump suggested Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth was the first to push for military action. Stay with IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East crisis.

The poll panel called the lapse a "clerical error," saying it was fixed immediately, and suspended an Assistant Section Officer over the incident.

One mistake could lead to annihilation: Scientists transport antimatterAntimatter cannot come into contact with regular matter, not even for a split second. If it does, both instantly destroy each other in a burst of energy.

Jalore, Baran among lowest in RBSE Class 5 pass rateRajasthan Board is set to declare the Class 5 and Class 8 results today, March 24, 2026. Once announced, students will be able to check their scores online through the official Shala Darpan portal, the link for which is rajshaladarpan.rajasthan.gov.in. More than 26 lakh students reportedly appeared for the RBSE Class 5 and Class 8 examinations this year and are now awaiting the announcement of their results.

Trump's 'keypad doctrine': How his phone shapes and entertains the worldFrom war threats to ceasefire pauses, US President Donald Trump's phone-led diplomacy has kept the world guessing, markets volatile and allies uneasy, raising questions over whether global security is being shaped by strategy or a stream of impulsive late-night posts.

Only Hindus, Sikhs and Buddhists can claim Scheduled Castes status: Supreme CourtIndividuals professing Hinduism, Sikhism or Buddhism are eligible for Scheduled Caste (SC) status, but if one converts to any other religion, it will result in the person losing SC status, the Supreme Court said.

You said, 'Let's do it': Trump shifts Iran war blame to Pete HegsethAs the Iran war drags on, Trump appears to rewrite how it began, pointing at his defence chief. But warnings, denials and shifting claims suggest a story far messier than he lets on.

Critics have called it a provocation, while others said she was being targeted for reciting mantras in a space meant for all faiths.

Indian man claims he was denied entry with family in Vietnam eatery, alleges racismThe clip, posted on Instagram by Daksh, captures a conversation with a man who claims he and his family faced discrimination while travelling in Vietnam.

sports.headline: Bengaluru stampede: What has RCB done to make Chinnaswamy safer for IPL 2026? IPL 2026: RCB have invested Rs 7 crore to overhaul crowd safety at Chinnaswamy Stadium, nearly a year after 11 fans died in a stampede during the team's trophy celebrations.

Klopp rubbishes Real links, leaves door open for return to football management Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp has strongly dismissed repeated speculation linking him with the managerial position at Real Madrid, labelling the reports as complete "nonsense" invented by "idiots".

BCCI bans match-day practice, announces stricter rules in updated guidelines IPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has expanded its operational guidelines for this season, introducing stricter regulations, including a complete ban on match-day practice sessions, according to a report by Cricbuzz.

Smriti Mandhana retains 2nd spot, Harmanpreet rises to 14th in ICC T20I rankings India batter Smriti Mandhana retained her second spot, while skipper Harmanpreet Kaur moved up to 14th in the latest ICC women's T20I rankings for batters on Tuesday.

RCB to keep 11 seats reserved in all matches as tribute to stampede victims Following a tragic stampede and the loss of hosting rights, RCB returns to M Chinnaswamy Stadium with a permanent 11-seat tribute and AI-driven safety protocols for the 2026 IPL season.

Rape accused Yash Dayal will not join RCB in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Rape accused Yash Dayal, who plays for the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, will not join the team this season, confirmed RCB's director of cricket, Mo Babat. Dayal was recently denied bail by a special POSCO court in Jaipur.

IPL 2026: Matthew Hayden picks GT star as heir to his iconic Mongoose bat IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans batting coach Matthew Hayden named Rahul Tewatia as the successor to his iconic Mongoose bat, praising the GT finisher's power-hitting ahead of IPL 2026.

KKR's injury crisis deepens, Akash Deep ruled out for the season IPL: Kolkata Knight Riders have been hit with yet another injury to a fast bowler. India pacer Akash Deep has been ruled out due to a lumbar stress injury.

Is this the make or break season for Hardik Pandya, the MI captain? IPL: Hardik Pandya's return to Mumbai Indians has not borne the results that it was supposed to. Can Hardik get out of Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav's shadow this season.

If Maradona was God at Napoli, then McTominay is Jesus: Argentine great's son Diego Maradona Jr sparked debate after declaring that if Diego Maradona was "God" at Napoli, then Scott McTominay is "Jesus", hailing the Scotland midfielder as the club's second-greatest player ever following his sensational rise in Serie A.

The Solidarity of the HopefulAt Patiala's National Institute of Sports, far from the glare of packed stadiums, India's throwers chase excellence in near anonymity. Here, success is forged not in moments of applause but in thousands of unseen repetitions, driven by a stubborn belief that today's effort will echo on a distant global stage.

Miami Open: Sinner breaks Novak Djokovic's record to script ATP Tour historyJannik Sinner created ATP Masters 1000 history on Monday evening at the Miami Open, registering a commanding 6-1, 6-4 win over Corentin Moutet at the Hard Rock Stadium.

Anushka Sharma earns maiden call-up as India announce squad for South Africa T20IsAnushka Sharma earned her first India call-up as the BCCI named the squad for the women's T20I series against South Africa starting April 17. Harmanpreet Kaur continued to lead the side, with Smriti Mandhana serving as her deputy.

Krunal Pandya sets sights on more IPL success with RCB: Just the beginningRCB all-rounder Krunal Pandya was instrumental in the franchise's maiden IPL triumph in 2025, delivering a string of match-winning performances. Yet, the seasoned campaigner remains hungry for more and has already set his sights on further glory.

Rinku Singh set to be appointed regional sports officer by UP governmentIPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' Rinku Singh is set to be named as the Regional Sports Officer by the Uttar Pradesh government. Rinku is being rewarded for his exceptional service with the Indian cricket team.

Ben Stokes breaks silence on Ashes drubbing: Hardest period of my captaincyEngland captain Ben Stokes has broken his silence after the team's humiliation at the hands of Australia in Ashes 2025-26. Stokes has called the series the hardest period of his captaincy journey.

MS Dhoni should be the CSK captain in IPL 2026: Aakash ChopraIPL 2026: Aakash Chopra said MS Dhoni should captain CSK if he plays IPL 2026. His comments reignited debate over Dhoni's role in the team.

AIFF women's chief seeks answers after Asian Cup blunders, kit row eruptsIPL 2026: Valanka Alemao demanded accountability after multiple lapses hit India's Asian Cup campaign. The controversy included ill-fitting jerseys and poor planning.

IPL 2026: KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane warns critics with rapid 58 in intra-squad clashIPL 2026: Ajinkya Rahane hit a rapid 58 in KKR's intra-squad clash. The knock came amid criticism over his captaincy ahead of IPL 2026.

Bumrah's words, painful lessons: Mayank Yadav's road to recovery for IPL 2026From skipping recovery sessions to surgery and back, Mayank Yadav opens up on his painful road to IPL 2026 and the conversations with Bumrah that kept him going.

Cristiano Ronaldo shares positive injury update, eases World Cup 2026 fearsCristiano Ronaldo shared a positive recovery update after his injury setback. The Portugal captain is expected to be fit for the 2026 World Cup.

Fined by Pakistan board, Sahibzada Farhan wins ICC award for T20 World Cup showOpener Sahibzada Farhan became the first Pakistan cricketer to win the ICC Player of the Month award since November 2024. Farhan scored 383 runs, including two hundreds, in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is not professional: Jitesh Sharma lifts lid on prodigyIPL 2026: Jitesh Sharma says Sooryavanshi is 'not professional' off the field. The reason? He can't stop eating ice cream at night. Cricket's brightest teen is just being a teenager.

PSL loses another star as Rajasthan Royals confirm Sam Curran's replacementIPL 2026: Dasun Shanaka replaced injured Sam Curran in Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 squad. The Sri Lankan all-rounder joined the franchise for Rs 2 crore after ditching PSL.

IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals await Mitchell Starc's NOC from Cricket AustraliaIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals are awaiting Cricket Australia's NOC for Mitchell Starc. The franchise remains hopeful he will join before their IPL 2026 opener.

Gavaskar blasts IPL overseas stars, urges teams to drop players for last-minute exitIPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar has called on IPL franchises to drop overseas players who pull out of tournament at the last minute, saying some are taking advantage of India's tradition of hospitality.

Hardik Pandya urged to step down as Mumbai captain ahead of new IPL seasonIPL 2026: Former India opener Kris Srikkanth has urged Hardik Pandya to relinquish the captaincy, saying Suryakumar Yadav, fresh from T20 World Cup success, should lead Mumbai Indians. Hardik has led MI since returning from Gujarat Titans in 2024.

Koneru Humpy withdraws from Candidates Tournament citing security concernsIndia's star chess player Koneru Humpy has withdrawn from the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament. Humpy's decision comes after she lashed out at the organisers over safety concerns in warn-torn West Asia.

KL Rahul will open in IPL 2026, confirms Delhi Capitals captain Axar PatelSenior batter KL Rahul will open the innings for Delhi Capitals, revealed the team management ahead of the team's IPL opener. Last season, Delhi played with an array of opening combinations, resulting in inconsistent performances.

Armed group warns foreign players to skip PSL, says Pakistan not safe for sportsAn armed group has warned overseas players to skip the 2026 edition of Pakistan Super League, citing a volatile security situation and calling Pakistan unfit for peaceful sporting activity.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
